Sidebar.title: Route Random
Sidebar.subtitle: A project made by
Sidebar.aboutDescription1: Route Random is a free random route generator that creates random circular routes for runners, joggers, and walkers. Generate random routes based on your desired distance and starting location anywhere in the world.
Sidebar.aboutDescription2: Perfect for discovering new paths, exploring your neighborhood, or finding new routes to keep your workouts exciting.

SeoSection.heading: Route Random | Free Running, Walking Route Generator
SeoSection.paragraph1: Hey, I am Lukas. I run a lot, and I got tired of repeating the same loops every week. That is why I built Route Random: a simple running route generator that creates random circular routes based on your distance and starting point. You can search an address or click on the map, and in a few seconds you get a fresh loop that usually brings you back close to where you started.
SeoSection.paragraph2: I kept the app practical on purpose. You can switch between distance and time, choose walking, running, or cycling, and tweak your route if you want more control. When the route looks good, you can export it as GPX or GeoJSON and use it in Garmin, Strava, or pretty much any fitness app or watch. No account wall, no endless setup, and no creepy tracking, just a quick way to find new roads when your usual route feels boring.
SeoSection.paragraph3: I originally made this for myself, but I decided to share it because other runners and walkers might find it useful too. Under the hood it uses OpenStreetMap, Leaflet, and OpenRouteService. If you want to try it or share it with a friend, all the links are below.
SeoSection.shareDescription: If Route Random helps you break out of your routine, feel free to share it.
SeoSection.shareTweetText: Check out this app: Route Random. It creates fresh circular running, walking, and cycling routes in seconds

LegendButton.normalDescription: The main part of your generated route.
LegendButton.overlapDescription: An overlapping section where your route goes back and forth. Sometimes a perfect loop isn't possible, so you may need to cover a section twice.
